paragraphs.body: From Dreams of the Cathedral to Reality
M-Point: This story explores how places can be different from what we imagine and how comparing two cultures can teach us about diversity and traditions.

annotations.comment: In the introduction, I would add a few more sentences to enrich the content of the introduction; for instance, you may pose questions, include an anecdote, or add a quote, etc.

paragraphs.body: Exposition: Before visiting St. Paul’s Cathedral in Pittsburgh, I imagined it as a tall and ancient building, with golden lights shining through colorful glass windows. I thought it would be a quiet and serious place, like the cathedrals I saw in movies. The name “St. Paul’s Cathedral” made me think of Europe and its old churches, full of history and solemn beauty. I expected to feel a sense of awe and silence as I walked through its grand doors.

annotations.comment: In the final draft, please remove the template.
annotations.context_right: Exposition: Before visiting St. Paul’s C

annotations.comment: In the final draft, please remove the template.

paragraphs.body: Image Description: A dreamy picture of a grand cathedral surrounded by golden light, with tall spires and colorful stained glass windows.

annotations.comment: The images did not look like AI-generated. What program did you use?

paragraphs.body: Conflict: When I finally visited St. Paul’s Cathedral, it was not as I had imagined. The building was beautiful, but the atmosphere felt different. Inside, the people were friendly, and the service was lively. It wasn’t as quiet and serious as I expected. I realized that my idea of a cathedral came from movies and books, not from real life. It surprised me how warm and inviting the place felt. Image Description: A realistic picture of a modern cathedral with people walking in and out, showing a warm and inviting place instead of a solemn one.

annotations.text: It wasn’t as quiet and serious as I expected. I realized that my idea of a cathedral came from movies and books, not from real
annotations.comment: You may consider using a mix of simple and complex sentences. For instance, "the Cathedral wasn't as quiet and serious as I expected; instead, I realized that my idea of a cathedral emerged not from real life, but from movies and books."

annotations.text: It surprised me how warm and inviting the place felt.
annotations.comment: I was surprised by how warm and inviting the place felt.

paragraphs.body: Rising Action:This made me think of Tengwang Pavilion (滕王阁) in Nanchang, my hometown. Tengwang Pavilion is also famous and grand, but it is full of life. You can hear the sounds of families, see people taking pictures, and even buy snacks from street vendors nearby. It stands tall with its traditional curved roof and red pillars. Compared to the cathedral, the Pavilion is more open and lively, showing Nanchang’s everyday culture instead of being just a historical monument. Both places are important landmarks, but the atmosphere around them is very different.

annotations.comment: Since this is a personal narrative, please use the first-person perspective consistently throughout the paper.
To avoid vague pronouns, I would specify what you mean by "this" - St. Paul's Cathedral.

paragraphs.body: Image Description: A colorful scene of Tengwang Pavilion, showing traditional Chinese architecture and people enjoying the lively surroundings. Climax:Thinking about both places, I saw how my ideas of Pittsburgh and Nanchang were influenced by stereotypes. I thought all cathedrals were serious and quiet, and I thought all historical places in China were the same. But this comparison showed me how different cultures celebrate history in their own ways. Both places taught me that a place’s real beauty comes from the people and the life around it. I learned that I should experience places with an open mind instead of relying on assumptions.

annotations.comment: celebrated - Please use the past tense consistently.

paragraphs.body: Image Description: A creative mix of cathedral spires and traditional Chinese pavilion roofs, showing the blend of two cultures.

Resolution:This experience helped me understand more about cultural and linguistic diversity. It also taught me not to make assumptions about places before visiting them. Nanchang and Pittsburgh are very different, but they both showed me how important it is to explore new places with an open mind. I now appreciate how each culture expresses its history through architecture and the way people interact with these spaces. Image Description: A symbolic image of a bridge connecting two cultures, with elements of both a modern cathedral and traditional Chinese architecture.

annotations.comment: Throughout the paper, please add non-English phrases/sentences from your mother tongue to enrich the content of the paper.
